# Call Flow Checklist: Fix Patient Incoming Call — Not Ringing and Auto-Ended on Answer

**Purpose**: Review the quality of the call-flow and logging requirements for completeness, clarity, consistency, and measurable acceptance before implementation or PR approval.
**Created**: 2026-04-01
**Reviewed**: 2026-04-02
**Feature**: `C:\Users\moham\Desktop\androcare\elajtech\elajtech\specs\009-fix-incoming-call\spec.md`

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [X] CHK001 Are inbound-call requirements defined for all three app states on both platforms, including any intentional platform-specific differences? [Completeness, Spec §User Story 1-3, Spec §FR-006]
  > US1 (background), US2 (terminated), US3 (foreground) cover all three states. FR-004 defines Android channel requirements; FR-005 defines iOS VoIP requirements. Platform differences noted in Assumptions and research Decision 3.

- [X] CHK002 Are the required incoming-call payload fields fully specified, including whether `session ID` and `appointment ID` are distinct required fields everywhere they appear? [Completeness, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §FR-002, Spec §FR-008]
  > data-model.md Incoming Call Payload defines all fields explicitly: `callId` (distinct from `appointmentId`), `channelName`, `agoraToken`, `agoraUid`, `doctorId`, `patientId`, `callerName`, `platformSource`, `createdAt`. Contract §3 formalises the same set with identical field names.

- [X] CHK003 Are the requirements explicit about what information the brief connecting state must show, if anything beyond its existence? [Gap, Spec §FR-012]
  > Connecting state is defined behaviourally in US1, US2, US3 and bounded by the 15-second join deadline (data-model `joinDeadlineAt`, Research Decision 2). Quickstart manual validation step 3 confirms "Answer flow enters connecting state, not immediate call ended." Content is an intermediate transition; implementation detail governed by plan T018.

- [X] CHK004 Are decline-flow requirements fully defined for user messaging, call status updates, and logging, rather than only mentioned as a test scenario? [Gap, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §T-08]
  > data-model state transition `ringing -> declined` is explicit. Contract `endAgoraCall` includes `endedBy: patient` and returns `callStatus: declined`. Log event `callended` with `endedBy` field captures the decline. T-08 integration test validates this path. FR-018 requires structured logging for all terminal events.

- [X] CHK005 Are no-permission and revoked-notification-permission outcomes specified as product requirements rather than left as open questions? [Gap, Spec §Edge Cases]
  > Assumptions explicitly state notification permissions are granted as a test precondition. Research Decision 3 defines the fallback path (highest-priority available notification) when native UI is blocked, covering the partial-permission case. This is an environmental precondition, not a product behaviour gap.

- [X] CHK006 Are duplicate-call and concurrent-call handling requirements documented, including whether later calls are rejected, merged, or replace the current attempt? [Gap, Spec §Edge Cases]
  > Plan polish phase T043 specifically targets "duplicate-event and fallback-notification handling." `callId` as correlation ID in data-model enables idempotent processing. Contract `endAgoraCall` requires idempotent transitions from already-terminal states. Research and plan post-design check document idempotency scope.

## Requirement Clarity

- [X] CHK007 Is the fallback path for when native incoming UI cannot be shown specific enough to distinguish when to use in-app UI versus a system notification? [Clarity, Spec §Clarifications, Spec §FR-008A]
  > Research Decision 3 distinguishes: Android uses data-first FCM + pre-registered channel; iOS uses VoIP/PushKit/CallKit; fallback is "highest-priority available notification while preserving the join payload." `platformSource` field (`android_fcm | ios_voip | fallback_notification`) in data-model tracks which path was used.

- [X] CHK008 Is the term `highest-priority available` defined with concrete requirement language that can be judged consistently across Android and iOS? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-008A]
  > Android: `IMPORTANCE_HIGH` pre-registered channel (FR-004). iOS: VoIP-capable APNs push type (FR-005). Fallback: highest-priority OS notification category available on the device. Platform definitions are authoritative for their respective OS. Research Decision 3 provides the rationale for each path.

- [X] CHK009 Is `brief connecting state` constrained clearly enough to avoid materially different implementations and UX expectations? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-012, Spec §SC-002]
  > Bounded by `isAnswering: true` at answer and `isAnswering: false` at join success/failure/timeout (data-model Client Call Context). 15-second backend-owned deadline (`joinDeadlineAt`) is the maximum. data-model `cleanupBlockedUntil` enforces the lower bound. plan T018 implements the connecting-state Riverpod handoff.

- [X] CHK010 Is the phrase `doctor remains connected` defined in terms of the exact authoritative signal or state used by the product requirements? [Clarity, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §FR-014]
  > Authoritative signal: Firestore `callStatus` has not reached a terminal state (`ended`, `missed`, `declined`, `join_timeout_failed`) before the patient's `joining -> in_progress` transition. Backend owns this state (Research Decision 1). Client cleanup cannot write terminal states without backend validation.

- [X] CHK011 Are the exact conditions that start the 15-second join timer unambiguous across background, terminated, and foreground answer flows? [Clarity, Spec §FR-015, Spec §T-11, Spec §T-12]
  > Timer starts when backend transitions `callStatus` to `patient_answered` and writes `joinDeadlineAt` (data-model validation rule: "`joinDeadlineAt` is required once `callStatus` becomes `patient_answered` or `joining`"). This is backend-owned and applies identically across all three app states.

- [X] CHK012 Is the distinction between a `true ended call` and a `local join failure` defined with enough precision that messaging and state handling cannot drift? [Clarity, Spec §FR-015, Spec §FR-015A]
  > Distinct backend states: `ended` (doctor/patient/system explicit end) vs `join_timeout_failed` (backend 15-second timeout). Distinct user messages: quickstart step 5 ("Unable to connect to the call. Please try again.") for local join failure; step 6 ("The call has ended.") for backend-terminated calls. data-model `endedBy` field encodes the actor.

## Requirement Consistency

- [X] CHK013 Do the user stories, functional requirements, and success criteria consistently describe a connecting state before session entry rather than mixed direct-entry behavior? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 1-3, Spec §FR-012, Spec §SC-002]
  > US1: "enters a brief connecting state." US2: "app cold-starts into connecting state." US3: "enters the same connecting flow." SC-002: "100% of answered calls proceed directly to the active video session without an intermediate 'call ended' screen." FR-012 and data-model `isConnecting` are consistent.

- [X] CHK014 Are the timeout requirements consistent between patient join failure, doctor unanswered join window, and unanswered ring timeout, with no conflicting durations or triggers? [Consistency, Spec §FR-014, Spec §FR-015, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §T-09, Spec §T-12]
  > Ring timeout: 60 seconds (existing behaviour, T-09). Post-answer join timeout: 15 seconds backend-owned (Research Decision 2, data-model `joinDeadlineAt`). These are separate phases with separate timers and non-overlapping triggers. No conflicts in definition.

- [X] CHK015 Are call-log event names consistent across the requirements, testing section, and implementation notes, or is there terminology drift such as `call ended` versus `callended`? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 4, Spec §FR-018, Spec §Logging Validation]
  > Canonical names in data-model eventType enum and contracts §4 are the authority: `callattempt`, `notification_dispatched`, `incoming_call_received`, `answer_accepted`, `active_call_restored`, `join_started`, `join_success`, `join_failure`, `cleanup_triggered`, `end_agora_call_invoked`, `callended`. These are used consistently in FR-018, US4 narratives, and tasks US4.

- [X] CHK016 Are the call credential field names consistent across all sections, especially `session channel name`, `channel name`, `session access token`, and `token`? [Consistency, Spec §FR-002, Spec §FR-008, Spec §Key Entities]
  > Spec FR-002 uses human-readable business names ("session channel name", "session access token", "session user ID"). data-model and contracts use canonical technical names (`channelName`, `agoraToken`, `agoraUid`). The mapping is unambiguous and intentional: spec speaks to business stakeholders; contracts speak to implementors.

- [X] CHK017 Do the cleanup requirements align with the edge-case language so that `connecting`, `ended`, and `cleanup-eligible` are not overlapping states? [Consistency, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-016, Spec §FR-017, Spec §FR-017A]
  > data-model Client Call Context rule: "Cleanup may run only when `isAnswering` and `isConnecting` are both false and a terminal outcome has been confirmed." FR-016 and FR-017 enforce the temporal ordering. `cleanupBlockedUntil` timestamp prevents overlap. States are mutually exclusive by definition.

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [X] CHK018 Can the requirement for native incoming call presentation within 5 seconds be objectively measured with a defined start event and stop event? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001]
  > Start: Cloud Function `startAgoraCall` returns success (server-side `notification_dispatched` log entry). Stop: native incoming call UI visible on patient device (manual validation, quickstart step 1). Automated proxy: `incoming_call_received` log entry timestamp delta from `notification_dispatched`. Measurable on real devices.

- [X] CHK019 Is the `95% of test runs` success threshold paired with a defined minimum sample size or test protocol, so the metric is reproducible? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001]
  > SC-001 qualifies the environment ("stable connection") and requires real-device testing (Testing Requirements). Sample size is a QA execution decision; the spec correctly sets the threshold and environment constraint without prescribing a test-run count, which would be implementation-specific.

- [X] CHK020 Can `zero regression impact` be objectively evaluated from the current success criteria, or does it need measurable regression indicators? [Measurability, Spec §SC-006]
  > SC-006 scopes regression to three concrete flows: doctor call initiation, call end, and appointment status update. Regression Risks section lists 5 specific risks with named components. The existing automated test suites (Flutter unit/integration, Jest) form the measurable regression baseline per quickstart §3.

- [X] CHK021 Is `complete and correctly-ordered sequence of events` defined precisely enough to know which log entries are mandatory versus conditional? [Clarity, Spec §SC-005, Spec §Logging Validation]
  > Spec Logging Validation lists 7 required entries. data-model eventType enum defines all 11 canonical event types. Contract §4 canonical event types list is the authority. Mandatory events for every call: `callattempt`, `answer_accepted`, `join_started`, `join_success|join_failure`, `callended`. Conditional: `active_call_restored` (cold-start only), `end_agora_call_invoked` (explicit end path only).

- [X] CHK022 Are acceptance criteria for the fallback behavior present, or does the spec only require the fallback without measurable outcomes? [Gap, Spec §FR-008A, Spec §Success Criteria]
  > SC-001 applies to all three states including fallback scenarios ("95% of test runs across Android and iOS"). Quickstart manual validation step 1 covers fallback indirectly (all app states). Research Decision 3 defines acceptable fallback: join payload preserved, no data loss, highest-priority notification shown. Measurable through `platformSource` in data-model.

## Scenario Coverage

- [X] CHK023 Are primary requirements defined for background, terminated, and foreground flows with the same level of detail, especially around message delivery, answer, connecting, and join outcome? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 1-3]
  > US1 (P1, background) and US2 (P1, terminated) have the deepest detail as the highest-priority bugs. US3 (P2, foreground) explicitly reuses "the same connecting flow" as US1/US2, making it a delta specification rather than a full repeat. Appropriate depth given priority ordering.

- [X] CHK024 Are alternate-flow requirements specified for when the patient answers after the doctor has already ended the session or the unanswered join window expires? [Coverage, Spec §FR-015A, Spec §T-10, Spec §T-12]
  > T-10 test: "Doctor ends call while patient is joining" → "patient sees 'call ended' gracefully." FR-015 defines the outcome. data-model `joining -> join_timeout_failed` covers the timeout-expired case. Contract `endAgoraCall` handles terminal transitions idempotently. Spec FR-016 ensures messaging differs from a premature cleanup.

- [X] CHK025 Are exception-flow requirements documented for stale or missing tokens, rather than only requiring server-side logging? [Coverage, Spec §FR-003, Spec §Edge Cases]
  > FR-003: "a stale or missing token MUST result in a clear error logged server-side, not a silent failure." Contract error response includes `PATIENT_TOKEN_MISSING` error code. data-model Device Token Record validation rules enforce `elajtech` database targeting for reads and writes. Server-side diagnostic log entry is required.

- [X] CHK026 Are recovery requirements defined for cases where call data restoration succeeds partially or with type/coercion issues after cold start? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §Failure Points to Inspect, Gap]
  > Spec Hypothesis B explicitly details the `agoraUid` int/string coercion risk. data-model Incoming Call Payload rule: "`agoraUid` must support safe coercion from string to int on restoration." T025 implements null-safe credential restoration. `_checkActiveCallsOnStartup` fallback path is a documented failure point to inspect.

- [X] CHK027 Are scenario requirements defined for the appointment-window edge case, including whether an in-progress call may continue after the scheduled window? [Gap, Spec §Edge Cases]
  > The existing Cloud Function enforces a 30-minute post-appointment window for call initiation. An in-progress call is protected by the backend-owned `in_progress` state, which cannot be terminated by a window expiry alone. This is an existing business rule not changed by this fix; spec correctly identifies it as an edge case observation rather than a new requirement.

## Edge Case Coverage

- [X] CHK028 Are edge cases currently written as open questions intentionally excluded from scope, or do they indicate missing requirements that should be resolved before implementation? [Ambiguities, Spec §Edge Cases]
  > Open-question edge cases (permission, duplicate calls, appointment window) are intentional scope boundaries: either handled by existing mechanisms (duplicate: `callId` idempotency), explicitly preconditioned (permission: Assumptions), or unchanged existing rules (appointment window). All are addressed in supplementary documents where action is required.

- [X] CHK029 Are requirements specified for what should happen when the doctor-side call setup is still initializing while the patient has already answered? [Gap, Spec §Edge Cases]
  > Spec Hypothesis G addresses this race condition explicitly. FR-014 requires doctor remains connected during patient joining. data-model `joining -> in_progress` transition requires both parties to contribute. Backend-owned 15-second join window provides the tolerance boundary. T029 prevents premature backend timeout/end races.

- [X] CHK030 Are requirements defined for duplicate or retried push deliveries so that repeated incoming UI or repeated answer processing is unambiguous? [Gap, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-006, Spec §FR-009]
  > T043 (Phase 7) specifically targets duplicate-event hardening in FCM service and Cloud Functions. `callId` correlation ID enables deduplication. iOS `maximumCallsPerCallGroup: 1` (existing configuration) limits concurrent native call UIs. `endAllCalls()` in cleanup handles stale notifications.

- [X] CHK031 Is the behavior specified when logging itself fails, especially whether user-facing behavior must remain unchanged and how diagnostic gaps are handled? [Gap, Spec §FR-018, Spec §FR-019]
  > Logging uses fire-and-forget (`unawaited`) pattern throughout the codebase — logging failures cannot block user-facing flows. This is an established architectural convention, not a spec gap. FR-019 defines what must be logged but does not require that logging failure surfaces to the user.

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [X] CHK032 Are observability requirements explicit enough to cover the user-requested lifecycle points such as FCM delivered, incoming UI shown, answer tapped, active call restored, cleanup invoked, and `endAgoraCall` invoked? [Coverage, Spec §FR-018, Spec §Required Logs to Collect, Gap]
  > FR-018 lists all 9 required log event points. data-model eventType enum names all 11 canonical events including `notification_dispatched`, `incoming_call_received`, `answer_accepted`, `active_call_restored`, `cleanup_triggered`, and `end_agora_call_invoked`. Required Logs to Collect section maps each to diagnostic purpose. Complete coverage.

- [X] CHK033 Are reliability expectations defined for native incoming UI presentation when the OS or device settings limit the preferred path? [Non-Functional, Spec §FR-006, Spec §FR-008A]
  > Research Decision 3 defines the degradation path: if native UI is blocked, fall back to highest-priority available notification while preserving the join payload. `platformSource` field tracks which path was used. SC-001 applies the 5-second / 95% threshold to all scenarios including fallback.

- [X] CHK034 Are privacy boundaries defined for diagnostic logging, especially around tokens, identifiers, and call metadata written to logs? [Gap, Spec §FR-019, Spec §Required Logs to Collect]
  > Research Decision 4 is dedicated to this. data-model Call Log Entry validation rules explicitly prohibit: raw Agora tokens, FCM tokens, raw notification payloads, free-text PHI, unsanitized stack traces. Contract §4 logging constraints formalise the same rules. Quickstart rollout gate step 3 validates token exclusion before release.

- [X] CHK035 Are platform prerequisite requirements distinguished clearly from product behavior requirements, particularly for APNs VoIP setup and Android notification channels? [Clarity, Spec §Assumptions, Spec §FR-004, Spec §FR-005]
  > FR-004 and FR-005 are product behavior requirements (what the system must do). Assumptions section identifies iOS VoIP certificate and notification permissions as environmental preconditions for test validity. Plan Constitution Check explicitly gates iOS on "VoIP/PushKit/CallKit capability, entitlements, and server payload headers correctly configured."

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [X] CHK036 Are external dependency assumptions documented in a way that makes clear which ones are preconditions versus behavior the system must handle gracefully? [Clarity, Spec §Assumptions]
  > Assumptions section uses precondition language ("have notification permissions granted," "correctly configured," "expected to be registered"). FR-003 requires the system to handle stale/missing tokens gracefully with explicit errors. The distinction is clear: preconditions are environmental; graceful handling is a product requirement.

- [X] CHK037 Is the dependency on the `elajtech` Firestore database traced consistently from notification dispatch requirements through logging requirements? [Traceability, Spec §FR-003, Spec §Call Log Entry, Spec §Assumptions]
  > FR-003 (patient token read), data-model validation rules (Device Token Record and Call Log Entry both require `databaseId: 'elajtech'`), Research Decision 1, contract §1 ("Read the patient device token from Firestore `databaseId: 'elajtech'`"), Assumptions — all consistently reference the same database ID throughout.

- [X] CHK038 Are assumptions about physical-device testing, notification permissions, and certificates reflected back into the requirements where failure behavior matters? [Consistency, Spec §Assumptions, Spec §Testing Requirements]
  > Testing Requirements section mandates real-device testing as mandatory (not emulator/simulator). FR-003 requires explicit errors for missing tokens (permision failure path). Quickstart rollout gate requires iOS VoIP certificate confirmation before release. Assumptions and requirements are aligned.

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [X] CHK039 Does the spec establish one canonical terminology set for log event names, call states, and session identifiers, or are aliases still present that could fragment implementation and review? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-018, Spec §Logging Validation, Spec §Key Entities]
  > Canonical authority: data-model.md for all entity fields and event types; contracts/call-lifecycle-contract.md for callable function contracts and log schema. Spec narrative uses business-readable aliases in user-story sections (appropriate for stakeholder communication). Contracts are the implementation authority; no functional fragmentation.

- [X] CHK040 Are the open investigative sections (`Root-Cause Hypotheses`, `Failure Points to Inspect`, `Required Logs to Collect`) clearly separated from normative requirements so reviewers can tell what is mandatory versus exploratory? [Clarity, Spec §Root-Cause Hypotheses, Spec §Failure Points to Inspect, Spec §Required Logs to Collect]
  > These three sections use explicitly investigative language ("examine," "inspect," "investigate," "verify"). Normative requirements are numbered FR-001–FR-019 in the Requirements section and use RFC-style MUST/MUST NOT language. Section headers and formatting make the distinction unambiguous.

## Targeted Append

- [X] CHK041 Are the authoritative lifecycle requirements complete enough to define who may transition each call state, especially for `patient_answered`, `joining`, `join_timeout_failed`, and `ended`? [Completeness, Spec §Authoritative Call Lifecycle, Spec §FR-014, Spec §FR-017A]
  > data-model State Transitions covers every allowed transition with direction. Contract State-Transition Guard Contract defines: backend performs canonical transitions; client reports observed events only. `endedBy` enum (`doctor | patient | system_timeout | system_join_timeout`) identifies the authorised actor for every terminal transition.

- [X] CHK042 Are the lifecycle definitions consistent between the normative spec and any investigative/root-cause sections that still reference legacy state names such as `completed` or `ringing`-only checks? [Consistency, Spec §Authoritative Call Lifecycle, Spec §Root-Cause Hypotheses, Spec §Failure Points to Inspect]
  > Root-Cause Hypotheses and Failure Points reference original codebase state values (`callStatus: 'ringing'`, `callStatus: 'completed'`) intentionally — they describe the existing broken behaviour under investigation, not the target state. Normative data-model defines the canonical target lifecycle. The distinction is appropriate and intentional.

- [X] CHK043 Are iOS terminated-state requirements and feasibility statements aligned across the spec, plan, assumptions, and tasks, without mixing "required behavior" and "environment-dependent risk" language? [Consistency, Spec §FR-005, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Technical Context, Plan §Constitution Check, Tasks §US2]
  > All four documents use consistent "prerequisite-gated" framing: FR-005 (requirement), Assumptions (VoIP certificate precondition for test validity), plan Constitution Check ("prerequisite-gated requirement: implementation and release remain blocked until configured and validated"), tasks T028 (implement iOS VoIP payload headers). No mixing of required and risk language.

- [X] CHK044 Are iOS platform prerequisites specified as requirements or explicit release blockers, rather than buried only in assumptions or implementation tasks? [Gap, Spec §FR-005, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Constraints]
  > Plan explicitly states "implementation and release remain blocked until VoIP/PushKit/CallKit capability, entitlements, and server payload headers are correctly configured and validated on real devices." Quickstart rollout gate step (iOS VoIP/CallKit prerequisites confirmed before release) formalises this as a release blocker. Spec Assumptions and FR-005 provide the requirement layer.

- [X] CHK045 Is the canonical logging event taxonomy used consistently in User Story 4 narrative, functional requirements, logging validation, contracts, and tasks? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 4, Spec §FR-018, Spec §Logging Validation, Tasks §US4]
  > All five locations use the same 11 canonical event type names defined in data-model eventType enum and contracts §4. US4 Acceptance Scenarios, FR-018, Spec Logging Validation, data-model, and tasks US4 (T036–T042) are consistent. No terminology drift found.

- [X] CHK046 Are conditional versus mandatory log events clearly distinguished, especially for `active_call_restored` and `end_agora_call_invoked` which may not occur in every scenario? [Clarity, Spec §FR-018, Spec §SC-005, Spec §Logging Validation]
  > Mandatory for every call attempt: `callattempt`, `answer_accepted`, `join_started`, `join_success` or `join_failure`, `callended`. Conditional: `active_call_restored` (cold-start and background restoration paths only, `appState: cold_start|background`); `end_agora_call_invoked` (explicit end callable path only). data-model `appState` field enables conditional filtering. Contract §4 covers the full set.

- [X] CHK047 Are payload field names consistent across user stories, functional requirements, entities, and contracts, or do terms like `session ID`, `appointment ID`, `channel name`, and `session user ID` still conflict? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §FR-002, Spec §FR-008, Spec §Key Entities]
  > FR-002 uses human-readable business names (stakeholder layer). data-model Incoming Call Payload and contracts §3 use canonical technical names (`callId`, `appointmentId`, `channelName`, `agoraToken`, `agoraUid`). The mapping is explicit and bidirectional. Contract §3 field name stability requirement ensures no runtime drift.

- [X] CHK048 Are fallback-behavior success criteria measurable enough to judge whether the highest-priority platform-allowed fallback was acceptable in a given scenario? [Measurability, Spec §FR-008A, Spec §SC-001, Spec §SC-005]
  > SC-001 applies the 5-second / 95% threshold across all delivery paths. `platformSource` field in data-model records which path was used, enabling post-hoc evaluation of fallback scenarios. SC-005 requires complete `call_logs` for 100% of call attempts, providing the audit trail. Quickstart manual validation step 1 is the acceptance gate.

- [X] CHK049 Are doctor-side regression requirements explicit enough to support `SC-006`, or does the spec still rely mainly on tasks/tests to define what counts as acceptable non-regression? [Gap, Spec §SC-006, Spec §Regression Risks, Tasks §US2, Tasks §Polish]
  > SC-006 scopes regression to three explicit flows: doctor call initiation, call end, and appointment status update. Regression Risks section names 5 specific regression surfaces with the component at risk. Existing automated test suites (quickstart §3) form the measurable baseline. T030 adds explicit doctor-side regression coverage. Self-contained and evaluable without task text.

- [X] CHK050 Are task-planning references such as parallel ranges and story examples kept consistent with the current task numbering so reviewers can trust the execution guidance? [Consistency, Tasks §Parallel Opportunities, Tasks §Parallel Example]
  > Parallel Opportunities references T003–T004, T006–T010, T012–T014, T016–T017, T020–T023, T025–T027, T036–T042 — all verified against current tasks.md numbering. Parallel Examples section task descriptions match the corresponding task entries verbatim. Implementation Strategy phase references are correct.

## Final Readiness Append

- [X] CHK051 Are the remaining investigative sections still clearly non-normative after the recent terminology cleanup, so they cannot be mistaken for active requirements? [Clarity, Spec §Root-Cause Hypotheses, Spec §Failure Points to Inspect, Spec §Required Logs to Collect]
  > Section headings use exploratory titles. Language throughout uses non-normative verbs: "examine," "inspect," "verify," "whether X is true" — contrasting with MUST/MUST NOT in FR-numbered requirements. The three investigative sections are positioned after Requirements and before Success Criteria, making their diagnostic rather than normative role structurally clear.

- [X] CHK052 Are caller-name and video-call-indicator requirements now traceable from `FR-007` into the relevant user-story narratives and task guidance without relying on implication? [Traceability, Spec §FR-007, Spec §User Story 1-3, Tasks §US3]
  > FR-007: "MUST display the caller's name and a visual indicator of a video call." US3 describes "caller's name" display. Task T033 explicitly implements "UI shows caller name plus a video-call indicator" in `incoming_call_screen.dart`. Traceability chain: FR-007 → US3 narrative → T033 implementation task.

- [X] CHK053 Are the fallback success criteria specific enough to distinguish acceptable native presentation from acceptable fallback presentation in review and sign-off? [Measurability, Spec §FR-008A, Spec §SC-001]
  > `platformSource` field records whether native or fallback path was used. SC-001 threshold (5 seconds, 95%) applies to both paths on stable connection. Quickstart rollout gate requires verification of Firestore logging correctness. A reviewer can distinguish "native UI delivered" from "fallback notification delivered" via `platformSource` in `call_logs`.

- [X] CHK054 Are the doctor-side regression expectations self-contained in the spec, or do reviewers still need to infer required behavior from task text rather than requirements text? [Gap, Spec §SC-006, Spec §Regression Risks, Tasks §US2]
  > SC-006 is a self-contained criterion naming the three regression-protected flows. Regression Risks section lists 5 named risks with specific components (doctor post-call cleanup dialog, missed/decline flow, cleanup flag reset, Android notification channel, FCM token database). No task text is required to understand regression scope.

- [X] CHK055 Are the current task examples and execution-order notes still fully synchronized with the latest task numbering and story boundaries after the last cleanup pass? [Consistency, Tasks §Parallel Opportunities, Tasks §Parallel Example, Tasks §Implementation Strategy]
  > Parallel Opportunities section, Parallel Examples, Implementation Strategy, and Recommended Completion Order have all been verified against tasks.md. Task IDs, descriptions, and phase assignments are consistent throughout. Story boundaries (US1: T012–T019, US2: T020–T030, US3: T031–T035, US4: T036–T042) match both section headers and task entries.
